content.full_text:
FPPC

MAY 21 10:29 AM '90

Hawkins, Delafield & Wood
6220 Canoga Avenue, Suite 400
Woodland Hills, California 91367

(818) 704-0195

Telecopier (818) 704-4729

67 WALL STREET
NEW YORK, NY 10005
(212) 820-9300

CITY PLACE
185 ASYLUM STREET
HARTFORD, CT 06103
(203) 275-6820

ONE GATEWAY CENTER
NEWARK, NJ 07102-5311
(201) 642-8564

May 18, 1990

Scott Hallibran
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Hallibran:

This request for informal advice is being submitted to the Fair
Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") in connection with our
firm's role as Special Counsel to the Inland Valley Development Agency (the
"Agency"). I am seeking guidance from the Commission with respect to
determining the jurisdictional boundaries of the Agency for the purposes of
filling disclosure statements in accordance with the Agency's proposed Conflict
of Interest Code, a copy of which is included for your review. Please be
advised that the Agency requests advice as soon as possible in order that it
can ensure that its Conflict of Interest Code is approved by the County Board
of Supervisors for San Bernardino County and becomes effective within the
required six month period. Please be further advised that I have previously
discussed this matter at some length with Mr. John Wallace of your office who
is familiar with the circumstances surrounding the formation of the Agency.
After explaining the facts as set forth below, Mr. Wallace's initial response
indicated that the jurisdictional boundaries of the Agency, which although not
specifically a redevelopment agency is nevertheless acting as a redevelopment
agency, would probably be interpreted as the boundaries of the project area
established by the Agency.

FACTUAL BACKGROUND

The Agency was formed pursuant to a Joint Powers Agreement which was
first executed on January 24, 1990 and which was subsequently amended on
February 12, 1990. The initial Agreement provided for the joint participation

[The image appears to be extremely faint/light, with only barely visible handwritten text at the top. The text is too illegible to transcribe with confidence. Most of the page is blank or contains text too faint to read reliably.]

Page 2


of the County of San Bernardino and the Cities of Colton and Loma Linda.
Pursuant to the amended Agreement dated February 12, 1990, the City of
San Bernardino also became a member of the Agency and a party to the
Agreement. At that time the Agreement was also substantially revised to
incorporate specific provisions regarding the exercise of powers of the
Agency. I have attached a copy of the Agreement for your review in connection
with consideration of this matter. Specifically, the Agreement provides that
the Agency is created as a joint powers authority established pursuant to
Government Code Section 6500, et seq., but has the ability to exercise
redevelopment powers in accordance with the recent adoption of Assembly
Bill 419 which added Health and Safety Code Section 33320.5 to the Community
Redevelopment Law. A copy of Assembly Bill 419 is also included for your
review. It is important to note that since the Agency is not a redevelopment
agency but is a joint powers authority it has broader powers than a typical
redevelopment agency. Subsection (b) of Health and Safety Code
Section 33320.5 which was added by Assembly Bill 419 provides:

"The legislative bodies for communities having territory
within, adjacent to or in proximity to a military facility
or installation described in subdivision (a) above may
create a separate joint powers agency pursuant to Chapter 5
(commencing with Section 6500) of Division 7 of Title 1 of
the Government Code, which shall have and exclusively
exercise powers of an agency in furtherance of the
redevelopment of a project area approved by the joint
powers agency. In addition to the powers of agency, the
joint powers agency so formed shall also act as the
legislative body and planning commission for all approvals
and actions required by this part of the legislative body
and planning commission for the adoption and implementation
of the redevelopment plan. However, all land use,
planning, and development decisions with regard to the land
within the project area shall continue to be under the
control and jurisdiction of each of the respective local
legislative bodies or planning commissions, as applicable."

As noted previously, the Agency is comprised of representatives from
the County of San Bernardino and the Cities of Colton, Loma Linda and San
Bernardino (the "Member Jurisdictions"). Pursuant to Section 2.09 of the
Agreement, the Governing Board of the Agency is comprised of three (3) City
Council Members from the City of San Bernardino, two (2) Supervisors
representing the County of San Bernardino and two (2) City Council members
from the Cities of Colton and Loma Linda. The Agreement also provides for
alternates for each Member.

With respect to the territorial boundaries of the project area to be
formed by the Agency, Assembly Bill 419 provides that the Project Area may
include any areas within three (3) miles of the boundaries of Norton Air Force
Base to the extent such areas are not already part of an existing
redevelopment project area. Specifically, subsection (c) of Health and Safety
Code Section 33320.5 provides:

Page 3

"(c) The territory included within the project and
project area may be contiguous or noncontiguous, and any
project area may be located in whole or in part within one
or more of the communities impacted by the closure of the
military facility or installation, and the land to be
included within the project area within the community or
communities in proximity to the military facility or
installation shall be found necessary for the effective
redevelopment of the military facility or installation and
the adjacent area. A project area shall not include
territory outside the jurisdiction of the communities that
are parties to the joint powers agency without the consent
of the legislative body having jurisdiction over the
territory proposed to be included within the project area."

The Agency is currently in the process of forming a project area
which conforms to the requirements set forth above with each of the Member
Jurisdictions contributing portions of its own territory for inclusion in the
final project area.

In connection with the Agency's formation and the implementation of
redevelopment activities, the Agency has created its own Conflict of Interest
Code and, in connection therewith, is seeking guidance with respect to
determining the appropriate jurisdictional boundaries of the Agency for the
purposes of having the Governing Board, and any alternates, file the
appropriate Disclosure Statements.

It is our understanding from my conversations with Mr. Wallace that
under a typical redevelopment analysis, the redevelopment project area
boundaries would most likely be treated as the jurisdictional boundaries of a
redevelopment agency in terms of whether or not any conflicts of interest
existed. This analysis is apparently based upon the Commission's Rotman
advice letter pertaining to the disclosure requirements for a project area
committee. In said letter it was determined that the conflicts of interest
requirements only necessitated disclosure of interests located specifically
within the boundaries of the project area. We have further been informed by
Mr. Wallace that he is aware of no decisions specifically pertaining to the
filing of disclosure statements of redevelopment agency members.

In terms of determining the jurisdictional boundaries of the Agency
for disclosure purposes, it should be noted that any redevelopment agency may,
under certain circumstances, exercise its redevelopment powers outside of the
boundaries of a redevelopment project area to the extent that the
redevelopment agency determines that its activities would be of benefit to the
redevelopment project area. Accordingly, the Agency in this case has similar
powers, pursuant to the provisions pertaining to low- and moderate-income
housing, a redevelopment agency is specifically authorized by
statute to undertake low- and moderate-income housing projects outside of a
redevelopment project area's boundaries so long as such projects are
undertaken within the boundaries of the community which adopted the
redevelopment plan. Section 4.01 of the Agreement specifically describes the

[The image appears to be mostly blank/white with some very faint text visible on the right side that is too illegible to accurately transcribe]

Page 4


jurisdictional boundaries of the Agency as the "Project Area" which by
definition includes territory from each of the Member Jurisdictions.
Section 4.04 goes on to provide that projects may be undertaken within the
territorial boundaries of the Member Jurisdictions which may include areas
outside of the Project Area.

If the Agency's boundaries, for disclosure purposes, were to be
interpreted to include the boundaries of each of the Member Jurisdictions
then, since the County of San Bernardino (the "County") is one of the Member
Jurisdictions, the jurisdictional boundaries of the Agency would include the
entire unincorporated territory of the County and all of the territory of
the Member Jurisdiction cities. On the other hand, if the Rotman decision is
relied upon, it is conceivable that the disclosure statements need only be
executed with respect to any properties or interests held by the Governing
Board members within the actual boundaries of the project area formed by the
Agency and within a two (2) mile radius thereof. Such an analysis may suffice
since it is only on rare occasions, if at all, that the Agency would exercise
its redevelopment powers outside of the project area and to the extent that
such activities did take place, any members required to vote on such
activities would still be required to refrain from participating pursuant to
Government Code Section 87100, et seq., to the extent that any conflicts of
interest were involved.

QUESTION PRESENTED:

     Please provide advice as to the jurisdictional boundaries of the
Agency for the purposes of filing disclosure statements and for the purposes
of requiring the filing of such disclosure statements in connection with the
Agency's Conflict of Interest Code.

     As Special Counsel to the Agency, I respectfully request the
Executive Director of the Commission to review the foregoing analysis and
provide guidance to me in connection with this request for informal advice.
If I may be of any further assistance to your office in connection with this
request for advice, please do not hesitate to contact me at your earliest
convenience.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        HAWKINS, DELAFIELD & WOOD

                                        [signature]
                                        Aidee de Bortnowski

ADB:lw
VVDA0001/35758
cc:     Mr. Earl Goodwin

As Special Counsel to the Agency, I respectfully request the Executive Director of the Commission to review the foregoing analysis and provide guidance to me in connection with this request for informal advice. If I may be of any further assistance to your office in connection with this request for advice, please do not hesitate to contact me at your earliest convenience.
